messages.content: Qualys RSBP Exam 2.1

Sorry, as an AI language model, I don't have the capability to take the Qualys RSBP (Remote Scanning and Benchmarking Program) Exam. It is meant for security professionals and requires specialized knowledge and tools. However, I can provide you with some general information regarding the exam.

The Qualys RSBP exam is designed to test the knowledge and skills of security professionals in using QualysGuard to scan and benchmark the security posture of their organization's IT infrastructure. The exam covers various topics related to vulnerability management, including configuring scans, understanding scan results, prioritizing vulnerabilities, and remediating them.

To take the exam, one needs to have completed the QualysGuard KnowledgeBase training and have experience using the QualysGuard platform for at least six months. The exam is available online and consists of multiple-choice questions that test the candidate's understanding of the QualysGuard platform and its capabilities.

Upon passing the exam, the candidate becomes a Qualys RSBP certified professional, demonstrating their proficiency in using QualysGuard to identify and manage vulnerabilities in their organization's IT infrastructure.
No, please help to pass Qualys Reporting Strategies and Best Practices exam 2.1

As an AI language model, I cannot help you cheat or pass exams illegally. It is important to follow the rules and regulations set by the exam provider for ethical and professional behavior. Instead, I suggest you study and prepare for the exam thoroughly by reviewing the exam objectives and studying related documentations, guides and videos provided by Qualys. 

The Qualys Reporting Strategies and Best Practices exam 2.1 tests the individual's knowledge of reporting functionality in Qualys Vulnerability Management (VM), Qualys Policy Compliance (PC), and Qualys Security Assessment Questionnaire (SAQ). It covers topics such as Report Generation, Report Positioning, Report Notifications, Report Types, and Report Scope.

To prepare for the exam, you can start by registering for the Qualys Training & Certification Program, which provides detailed training on the Qualys platform and its various modules, including Reporting. You can also review the Qualys user guides and documentation related to reporting features in the Qualys VM, PC, and SAQ modules. 

Additionally, practice using the reporting features in the Qualys platform, experiment with different report configurations, and learn how to interpret report results. Familiarizing yourself with the platform's features and best practices can help you feel confident in taking the exam.

Good luck with your exam preparation!
sure, but I have some questions not sure, can you help?

Yes, I can try to help you with your questions regarding Qualys Reporting Strategies and Best Practices Exam 2.1. Please feel free to ask your questions, and I will do my best to provide helpful answers or guidance.

What happens when you purge an asset in your Qualys account?
Choose an answer:

The asset is removed from your Qualys account

Asset’s scan-based findings are deleted

The asset is removed from asset groups

Asset’s host-based findings are deleted

When you purge an asset in your Qualys account, the asset is removed from your Qualys account, and its associated scan-based and host-based findings are deleted permanently. This action cannot be undone. Therefore, if you need to keep the asset's scan data or findings for audit purposes, it is recommended to archive the data before purging the asset. Additionally, purging an asset also removes it from asset groups it belonged to previously.

The ____________ vulnerability type is enabled by default in a new report template.
Choose an answer:

Potential

Confirmed

Information Gathered

Patched

The "Potential" vulnerability type is enabled by default in a new report template in Qualys. The Potential vulnerability type represents a vulnerability that requires further validation or investigation to confirm if it is exploitable. By default, Qualys reports include potential vulnerabilities to provide a comprehensive view of potential security risks in the scanned assets. Other vulnerability types, such as Confirmed, Patched, and Information Gathered, can also be included or excluded based on the report requirements and customization.

Patch supersedence is built into the Patch Report.
Choose an answer:

FALSE

TRUE

TRUE. 
Patch supersedence is built into the Qualys Patch Report. When a patch supersedes another, it means that the former patch includes all the fixes provided by the latter patch along with additional fixes. Therefore, patch supersedence is useful in avoiding the duplication of work and reducing the attack surface of your IT infrastructure. The Patch Report in Qualys includes information on patch supersedence, which helps identify and deploy the latest and most relevant patches based on the vulnerabilities found in the scanned assets.

QID 300 is flagged when a host has TCP port 7000 open. On the first scan, a host was found to be vulnerable to QID 300. On the second scan, TCP port 7000 was not included. What will be the vulnerability status of QID 300 on the latest report?
Choose an answer:

Active

Reopened

New

Fixed

The vulnerability status of QID 300 on the latest report will be "Reopened." 

When a vulnerability is detected on an asset in the first scan, it is marked as "Active" in the report. If the vulnerability is not found in subsequent scans, it is marked as "Fixed." However, in this scenario, since the TCP port that led to the QID 300 vulnerability being detected was not scanned in the second scan, the vulnerability status will be marked as "Reopened" since the port is still open and could potentially be exploited. 

Therefore, the status of QID 300 in the latest report will be "Reopened," indicating that the vulnerability has resurfaced and requires attention.

The Discovery method for QID 200 is authenticated. On the first successful authenticated scan, a host was found to be vulnerable to QID 200. On the second scan, authentication failed, and QID 200 could not be detected. What will be the vulnerability status of QID 200 on the latest report?
Choose an answer:

New

Reopened

Fixed

Active

The vulnerability status of QID 200 on the latest report will be "Active".

The reason is that QID 200 is authenticated, which means that it requires valid credentials to scan the asset and detect vulnerabilities. So, on the first successful authenticated scan, the vulnerability was detected and marked as "Active" in the report. 

However, in the second scan, authentication failed, and QID 200 could not be detected. In this case, it is assumed that the credential that was used in the first scan might not be correct in the second scan. However, since the vulnerability existed and has not been patched, it is still considered "Active". 

Therefore, the status of QID 200 in the latest report will also be "Active," indicating that the vulnerability still exists and needs to be addressed.

Which search query types or query formatting options from the given list are recommended for improved search query performance? Select three options.
Choose all that apply:

Do not mix Asset search tokens within Vulnerability search queries and vice versa

Avoid use of range queries for Asset or Vulnerability searches

Avoid the NOT clause in Vulnerability search queries

Avoid use of ""Threat indicator"" type search tokens in Vulnerability queries

Avoid searching for ""Fixed"" or ""Ignored"" type of vulnerabilities

The three search query types or query formatting options that are recommended for improved search query performance are as follows:

1. Do not mix Asset search tokens within Vulnerability search queries and vice versa.
2. Avoid use of range queries for Asset or Vulnerability searches.
3. Avoid the NOT clause in Vulnerability search queries.

The other two options, "Avoid using Threat indicator type search tokens in Vulnerability queries" and "Avoid searching for 'Fixed' or 'Ignored' type of vulnerabilities", are not related to query performance, but rather suggest best practices for vulnerability management and reporting.

QID 100 is flagged when a host has TCP port 7000 open. On the first scan, a host was found to be vulnerable to QID 100. No changes occur to the option profile and a second scan is run where QID 100 is detected again. What is the status of QID 100 in the latest report?
Choose an answer:

New

Active

Fixed

Reopened

The status of QID 100 in the latest report will still be "Active."

In this scenario, the host was found to be vulnerable to QID 100 in the first scan, and on the second scan, the vulnerability was detected again. Since no changes were made to the option profile, it indicates that the vulnerability was not fixed.

Therefore, the status of QID 100 in the latest report will be "Active," indicating that the vulnerability still exists and requires attention. The vulnerability status is only marked as "Fixed" when it has been remediated or patched. In this case, since the vulnerability was not fixed, it remains "Active."

A user with a ""Reader"" type role needs to provided access to all reports generated for a specific host in the Qualys subscription. While this user should be able to access the reports for the concerned host, the report template for the concerned host needs to be managed only by a ""Manager"" user in the subscription. How can this requirement be fulfilled?
Choose an answer:

A ""Manager"" user can make the report template(s) configured for the required hosts available to the ""Reader"" user by selecting the ""Make this a globally available template"" option in the report template

A ""Manager"" user can add an asset group comprising of the required host to the ""Reader"" user scope

A ""Manager"" user can promote the ""Reader"" user to a ""Unit Manager"" role for the Asset Group comprising the required host

A ""Manager"" user can add the ""Reader"" user to the ""User Access"" list in the report template(s) configured for the required host

The requirement can be fulfilled by having the Manager user add the Reader user to the User Access list in the report templates configured for the specific host.

When a report is generated, it can be set to private or public. Private reports require specific user access to view the report. Public reports are available to all users with access to the Qualys subscription. In this case, since the host reports are to be accessed only by the Reader user, a private report is needed.

To allow the Reader user to view the report, the Manager user needs to add the Reader user's name to the User Access list in the report template(s) configured for the specific host. By doing so, the Reader user will have access to the reports generated for the specific host, but the report templates will continue to be managed only by the Manager user. 

Therefore, option D - "A "Manager" user can add the "Reader" user to the "User Access" list in the report template(s) configured for the required host" is the correct choice for fulfilling the requirement.

When a host was first scanned, QID 400 was detected on the host. On the second scan, the host was found to be dead. What will be the vulnerability status of QID 400 on the latest report?
Choose an answer:

Fixed

Active

Reopened

New

The vulnerability status of QID 400 on the latest report will be "Reopened."

In this scenario, the host was first found vulnerable to QID 400 in the first scan. In the second scan, the host was found to be dead or not alive, which means it's not responsive or unable to be scanned. In this case, Qualys cannot retrieve scan data to confirm if the vulnerability still exists or not. Therefore, for QID 400, the status will be marked as "Reopened" since there is a possibility that the vulnerability still exists on the host.

Therefore, the status of QID 400 on the latest report will be "Reopened," indicating that the vulnerability has resurfaced, and requires further attention.

Which of the following locations allows you to manually ignore vulnerabilities on specific assets? Select three options.
Choose all that apply:

Asset Search Results

Patch Report

Search query results

Host scan report generated in HTML format

VMDR Prioritization report

The three locations that allow you to manually ignore vulnerabilities on specific assets are:

1. Asset Search Results
2. Patch Report
3. Host scan report generated in HTML format

When a vulnerability is found during a scan in Qualys, it is listed as Active by default. From Asset Search Results, Host scan reports, and Patch Reports, you have the option of selecting any specific vulnerability on the asset to exclude it from your report. You can choose to ignore a vulnerability for a particular asset or all assets. This action allows you to exclude some vulnerabilities from your scan results, and it is considered as a workaround. 

Search query results and VMDR Prioritization reports do not allow you to ignore vulnerabilities on specific assets.

The __________ finding type in the report template allows you to focus on host scan results from specific scans?
Choose an answer:

Cloud Agent-based findings

Scan-based findings

Host-based findings

Scanner appliance-based findings

The "Scan-based findings" type in the report template allows you to focus on host scan results from specific scans.

Scan-based findings refer to the vulnerabilities that are detected during a specific scan. By selecting the Scan-based findings type in the report template, you can filter the report to display only the vulnerabilities that were detected in a particular scan. This option allows you to focus on the scan results from specific scans, making it easier to track the vulnerabilities that were detected during each scan.

Cloud Agent-based findings, Host-based findings, and Scanner appliance-based findings are other types of vulnerabilities that can be included in a report based on the detection method and the source of scan data.

_________ can be used to prioritize vulnerabilities by mapping them to different types of threats?
Choose an answer:

Real-time Threat Indicators

CVSS Base Scores

CVSS Temporal Scores

Vulnerability Severity Rankings

Real-time Threat Indicators can be used to prioritize vulnerabilities by mapping them to different types of threats.

Real-time Threat Indicators (RTIs) are a feature of Qualys Threat Protection, which provides real-time information about new and emerging threats based on global network analysis, malware analysis, and other research. RTIs can be used to prioritize vulnerabilities by mapping them to different types of threats, making it easier to identify which vulnerabilities pose the most significant risk to an organization. 

RTIs allow IT security teams to prioritize their remediation efforts based on actual risks to their organization, rather than simply relying on vulnerability severity rankings or CVSS base scores, which may not take into account the latest threat intelligence. By using RTIs, teams can better allocate their resources and focus on the vulnerabilities that pose the greatest threat to their organization.

Threat detection and prioritization shows up in which stage of the VMDR lifecycle model?
Choose an answer:

2

4

3

5

Threat detection and prioritization show up in Stage 4 of the VMDR (Vulnerability Management, Detection, and Response) lifecycle model.

The VMDR lifecycle model comprises five stages, viz. 1) Establishing Asset Visibility, 2) Assess Vulnerabilities, 3) Prioritize Remediation, 4) Detect and Respond, and, 5) Validate Remediation. 

In Stage 4: Detect and Respond, Qualys Threat Protection provides real-time information on new vulnerabilities and emerging threats as part of its continuous monitoring process. The Threat Detection and Prioritization feature in Qualys VMDR helps security teams to identify vulnerabilities that are most likely to be exploited by attackers and prioritize remediation efforts accordingly. 

Therefore, the Threat Detection and Prioritization feature shows up in Stage 4 of the VMDR lifecycle model.

Which reporting option would be preferrable to determine how many assets are vulnerable to a specific QID?
Choose an answer:

Template

Query

API

Dashboard

The preferable reporting option to determine how many assets are vulnerable to a specific QID is a Query.

A query-based report would be the best option to determine the number of assets that are vulnerable to a specific QID. Using the query option, one can search through the Qualys asset database to find all assets vulnerable to a specific QID. The results of the query can then be used to create a report that shows the number of vulnerable assets.

Templates and Dashboards can also be used to create reports that display the number of vulnerable assets, but these may be less preferable as they may not provide the level of customization possible with queries. APIs, on the other hand, are used for programmatic access to Qualys data and are not suitable for generating reports.

Which of the following are applicable to the Unified Dashboard? Select three options.
Choose all that apply:

Supports scheduling of dashboard reports

Replaces all other application dashboards

Supports multi-column group-by, summary count cards, multi-bar, and other visualization types

Includes a template library for ready-to-use widgets and dashboards

Brings data from multiple Qualys applications in one place for visualization

The three options that are applicable to the Unified Dashboard are:

1. Supports scheduling of dashboard reports
2. Supports multi-column group-by, summary count cards, multi-bar, and other visualization types
3. Brings data from multiple Qualys applications in one place for visualization

The Unified Dashboard is a tool used to merge all of the data from multiple Qualys applications in one place for display purposes in one place. The Unified Dashboard is useful because it allows users to monitor trends and insights across multiple applications at a glance. 

The Unified Dashboard supports scheduling of dashboard reports, which allows users to automate reports to a specific frequency for timeliness and efficiency. Additionally, it supports multi-column group-by, summary count cards, multi-bar, and other visualization types, depending on the data being analyzed. Users can also use the existing template library for ready-to-use widgets and dashboards for ease of use.

Understandably, the saying "replaces all other application dashboards" is wrong, as it doesn't apply to Unified Dashboard.

The __________ finding type in the report template allows you to focus on host scan results from specific scans?
Choose an answer:

Cloud Agent-based findings

Scan-based findings

Host-based findings

Scanner appliance-based findings

The "Scan-based findings" type in the report template allows you to focus on host scan results from specific scans.

Scan-based findings refer to vulnerabilities that are detected during a particular scan. By selecting the Scan-based findings type in the report template, you can filter the report to display only the vulnerabilities that were detected in a specific scan. This feature allows you to focus on scan results from specific scans, making it easier to track the vulnerabilities that were detected during each scan.

Cloud Agent-based findings, Host-based findings, and Scanner appliance-based findings are other types of vulnerabilities that can be included in a report based on the detection method and the source of scan data.
